task: The charset regex in get_encodings_from_content (utils.py) uses a non-greedy group. Simplify it to a plain greedy group.
correctness_argument: The non-greedy `(.+?)` stops at the first closing quote or `>`, capturing only the encoding token. Greedy `(.+)` would consume up to the last such character on the line, producing a garbage encoding string for any meta tag with trailing attributes or markup.
